purpose: This repository contains Infrastructure as Code (IaC) for the Cross River Bank Lending and Selling platform, provisioning and managing AWS cloud resources including Aurora databases, Airflow workflows, ECS Fargate tasks, S3 buckets, and other supporting services across multiple environments.

dataEntities.name: Loans
dataEntities.description: Core loan data stored in Aurora database

dataEntities.name: LoanFunding
dataEntities.description: Data related to loan funding process in dbt_temp schema

messagingPatterns.name: SQS queues for selling-x
messagingPatterns.description: Message queues for loan selling processes

techStack: AWS (ECS, Aurora, Lambda, S3, SQS, MWAA, Athena)

findings.title: Hard-coded certificate in repository
findings.description: A certificate file (crb.pem) is stored in the repository under docker/certs/. Certificates should never be stored in repositories and should instead be managed through secure certificate management solutions.

findings.title: Database credentials rotated but no integration tests
findings.description: The Lambda rotation functions change database passwords but don't have comprehensive tests to verify application functionality continues after rotation. This could lead to service disruption if applications can't connect with new credentials.

ambiguities: The exact purpose and functionality of the 'selling-x' component that uses SQS queues
The specific data models and schemas in the Aurora database beyond the mentioned 'app' and 'dbt_temp' schemas
The flow and interaction patterns between the various APIs (admin-api, web-api, ai-api)
What AI functionality is implemented in the ai-api Lambda functions
How the Airflow workflows integrate with the rest of the system beyond the sample DAG
